item.title: Time for another unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, online friends
sounds relaxing? not much of that hustle and bustle i would think
as is tradition
unhinged
this will torment me until the end of time
ok
man i wish i could eat that sand
don't have to imagine it, i've lived it! (not really)
mmm hmm
kind of a weird thing to make a whole book about
i'm a big pink floyd guy
pretty much all of my friends and myself had some variation of the bowl cut early-mid 90s
ah, Cleaver
i don't know anyone who doesn't love bagels
ah, the 6 genders
bet they wouldn't care either
especially you Chris
that's my set.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: BD-1 Cosplay Prop Build
item.description:
Using my Cake Day as an excuse to post this thing I meant to share a month ago! ?
I built this BD-1 as part of my Cal Kestis cosplay for Emerald City Comic Con. I didn't do the best job of taking WIP updates but here's what I have!
I started off with a tinfoil and wire chicken.
Cut out some craft foam shapes
Glued those shapes to the chicken
And then attempted using foam clay for the first time! It's a really neat product, but I thought the clay foam would be harder once dried. It was pretty squishy.
I continued building out the legs, and then decided I wouldn't get the right shape with foam clay so switched back to what I'm familiar with, craft foam.
I added side walls and blocked out the feet. They're not exactly like BD-1's but these are more practical for supporting the weight of the prop.
Feet and legs are (mostly) done!!
I added a slightly transparent panel to the back of the head like so
And then bought these super cheap LED lights. I'll definitely upgrade these in the future.
I added a green light to the back panel
Oooh shiny
And then built out the face with more craft foam and another LED
Added more of that panel from the back to the eye
And voila!
Now for my favorite part, the *tiny* details
Added antennae to the ears
They're just bits of wire with foam clay on the top and around the base!
More details!
Ready for paint!
Here he is, the bestest boi
I attached him with wires through the back of the chest plate. It worked for the day (I was con crunching this in a week don't judge me ?) but I definitely want to figure out a more stable mounting system for future conventions!
Tippy taps
And the final look. Thanks for reading and have a lovely day, friends!!
item.dc:creator: GingerSnark

item.title: i handwrote out the entire script to Lord Of The Rings
item.description: The Fellowship Of The Rings: Extended Edition Get a print of all my stuff here: http://www.linebylineposters.com
item.dc:creator: foxtooth

item.title: All of the world’s billionaires younger than 30 inherited their wealth, research has found.
item.description: The research by Forbes magazine found there were 15 billionaires aged 30 or under but that none had created their own wealth, instead benefitting from huge inheritances. Among them are the three children of Leonardo Del Vecchio, the founder of the luxury sunglasses company Luxottica. They became billionaires after his death in 2022. The 28-year-old Leonardo Maria (pictured above with his wife Jessica Serfaty Michel) and his two younger siblings each inherited a 12.5% stake in the family’s Luxembourg-based holding company Delfin, which owns nearly a third of the company behind the brands Ray-Ban and Oakley. The siblings have a fortune estimated to be worth $4.7bn each. The world’s youngest billionaire is Livia Voigt, 19, who has a $1.1bn fortune thanks to a 3.1% stake in WEG Industries, a Brazilian electrical equipment producer co-founded by her grandfather Werner Ricardo who died in 2016. https://www.theguardian.com/business/2024/apr/03/all-billionaires-under-30-have-inherited-their-wealth-research-finds
item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: Here is a collection of old images I have saved on my phone as my only form of interaction with other people.
item.description:
I always enjoy seeing a dump of memes, images and videos on imgur and I think most people on here enjoy it too. I want to post on here more but I usually end up deleting my post out of fear of being judged so I hope you like this.
I also saw someone leave some comments some images and that sounds fun so I am going to do that too.
I miss Chuck Norris jokes, they were so good.
I still haven't tried this.
Praise Cthulhu!
I have metal dice. I do feel very powerful.
Yes to all except outgoing.
Wow
I am part Czech and I approve of this message.
I have never got high and never will but this is such a funny scenario to me.
I still want one though.
I like to sleep a lot.
Hot cocoa > Tea and coffee
After trying tinder I agree.
Happens every time.
Monsters I tell you.
I still try it, worth a shot.
I still need to paint my figures.
Cat tax. These are older stuff I have saved from imgur so I hope some of you remember these. If not I hope you like anything new you haven't seen before. Maybe I will do one of these again if I get good feedback. Well thats all I got to say, have a nice day.
item.dc:creator: Haitharg

item.title: Your support means the world to me
item.description: I have been writing, recording, and releasing music online for several years now. I have shared it with friends and family before, but most of them don't really care. They respond, "Oh, that's cool", or something similar. I have had people kind of laugh at it before as well. I don't think they were trying to be mean, but everyone thinks you're trying to become famous if you put music out online, and they think I am being ridiculous for doing that. Like I am trying to become an influencer. That is never going to happen, and that is fine with me, I just want to share what makes me happy with people. So, I stopped sharing it with them, and I share it here now. The response here has been awesome, and I really appreciate the advice, and the people that continue to support me. It really does mean the world to me, and it is something I look forward to doing. Here is the latest song, number 64, here: https://youtu.be/n3MZieGYUhE?si=o65C5SQMPHMG_kVy
item.dc:creator: FromTheBeyond

item.title: Rainbow bridge
item.description: He hadn't been doing well these last few weeks. He's been slowly getting worse over the last 2 years after being diagnosed with liver cancer but this week in particular was bad. Most of the time, he'd refuse his kibble, but I hadn't given up on him because he still wanted to eat his favorite kinds of veggies so I'd mix them into his food to get him to eat a bit. Yesterday, he vomited 5 times so we gave him some water under his skin to make sure he didn't get dehydrated. Over night, he tried to go hide in the yard to sleep on his own several times which was uncharacteristic of him. I was planning on helping him go comfortably on monday if he didn't get better. Then I looked at him, behind me on my bed as I worked from home to take care of him and noticed he wasn't breathing. His body was still warm, but there was no heartbeat. He's gone.
item.dc:creator: astromancer
